description: Natural-language analytics over the Momentum Life claims gold layer.

glossary.NTU: Not Taken Up. A claim that was initiated (pre-lodge) but dropped off before formal lodgement. Tracked via the is_ntu flag and the ntu_funnel / ntu_at_risk views.
glossary.SLA: Service Level Agreement target of 20 days from lodge to decision. sla_breach = true when days_lodge_to_decision > 20.
glossary.occupation_mismatch: The occupation recorded at policy inception (occupation_at_inception) differs from the occupation stated at claim (occupation_at_claim). A disability-claim red flag that can affect claimability.
glossary.claim states: initiated (pre-lodge), lodged, in_assessment, decided. NTU claims never progress past initiated.
glossary.decision: The assessment outcome: pay, decline or refer. Blank/null means not yet decided.
glossary.drop_off_propensity: 0-1 score in ntu_at_risk blending days-outstanding (60%) and outstanding-requirement count (40%); higher = more likely to become NTU.
glossary.risk_score: 0-1 fraud/risk indicator on each claim; > 0.6 is treated as high-risk in the app.
glossary.sum_assured: Benefit amount in South African Rand (ZAR / R).

sample_questions: Which claims have an occupation mismatch?
Show me pre-lodge claims at risk of drop-off, sorted by propensity
What is the average cycle time for disability claims?
How many claims breached the 20-day SLA?
What is the NTU rate by claim type?
Show the decision split (pay / decline / refer) for death claims
Which requirement codes are most often outstanding?
Which assessor has the highest throughput?
List claims with a risk score above 0.6
How many claims are in each state by claim type?
